mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, generating positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, triggering negative short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of stable industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations outpace monetary policy adjustments, causing negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in over 65% of triggered cases, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with 60-day forward returns during periods of wage stagnation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates consumers are depleting savings to maintain spending, creating a fragile demand environment that drives negative equity returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month while RSAFS increases
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the divergence pattern shows no predictive power when housing inventory levels are high.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production coinciding with stable consumer prices signals healthy capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, generating positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL changes < 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism loses predictive accuracy when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
